title: Creating a Thread in Java: Two Primary Methods
description: A flowchart illustrating the two main ways to create and start a thread in Java, showing the steps for both extending the Thread class and implementing the Runnable interface.
relationships: Main Program chooses to extend Thread or implement Runnable
Thread Class is extended and run() method is overridden
Runnable Interface is implemented and run() method is defined
A Thread Object is created (passing a Runnable instance if applicable)
The Thread Object's start() method is called
start() requests the JVM Thread Scheduler to allocate resources
The Scheduler eventually calls the object's run() method on a new thread
